agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Larger organizations (higher total expenses) have more capacity to hire professional fundraising firms to sustain and expand their operations.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Thornton (2006) discusses the structural determinants of professional fundraising fees.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant but practically weak positive correlation was found. Nonprofits with higher total expenses spend more on professional fundraising fees, reflecting the institutionalization of fundraising as organizations grow.
